name: azure-communication-callingserver-java
description: Azure Communication Services CallingServer (legacy) Java SDK. Note - This SDK is deprecated. Use azure-communication-callautomation instead for new projects. Only use this skill when maintaining legacy code.
capabilities: Provides class name mapping between deprecated CallingServer and new CallAutomation SDKs
Shows Maven dependency changes for migration
Identifies deprecated features and their replacements
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 17 potential issues. All findings are false positives: (1) 'external_commands' triggered by markdown code blocks using backticks (`) - no shell execution present; (2) 'weak cryptographic algorithm' triggered by generic words like 'execute' in documentation. This is a documentation-only skill providing SDK migration guidance with no executable code.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers migrate from the deprecated Azure Communication CallingServer Java SDK to the new Call Automation SDK, ensuring legacy integrations continue working while enabling new features.
userTitle: Migrate from Azure CallingServer to Call Automation
limitations: Does not provide automated code migration tools
Does not include the new Call Automation SDK functionality
Does not support older SDK versions beyond the documented migration path
This SDK is deprecated - new projects should use azure-communication-callautomation

useCases.targetUser: Java developers maintaining Azure Communication Services integrations
useCases.title: Legacy SDK Migration

useCases.targetUser: Technical leads and architects planning Azure SDK migrations

useCases.targetUser: Java developers working with Azure Communication Services
useCases.title: Reference Guide
useCases.description: Developer looking up class name mappings and API changes between old and new SDK versions

bestPractices: Migrate to azure-communication-callautomation for new projects - the CallingServer SDK is deprecated
Review the trigger phrases ("callingserver legacy", "deprecated calling SDK") to activate this skill appropriately
Use this skill alongside the azure-communication-callautomation-java skill for complete migration guidance
antiPatterns: Do not use this SDK for new development - it is deprecated and will not receive updates
Do not mix CallingServer and CallAutomation classes in the same application
Do not assume ServerCall class exists in the new SDK - it was removed

faq.question: Is azure-communication-callingserver still supported?
faq.answer: No, this SDK is deprecated. Microsoft recommends using azure-communication-callautomation for all new projects.

faq.question: What is the main difference between the two SDKs?
faq.answer: The CallingServer SDK was renamed to Call Automation SDK with improved features and simplified class names.

faq.question: Can I use both SDKs in the same project?
faq.answer: It is not recommended to mix both SDKs. Migrate completely to Call Automation for consistency.

faq.question: Where do I find the new Call Automation SDK?
faq.answer: The new SDK is available as com.azure:azure-communication-callautomation on Maven Central.

faq.question: What happened to the ServerCall class?
faq.answer: ServerCall was removed. Use CallConnection for managing call operations in the new SDK.

faq.question: Does this skill help with new Call Automation features?
faq.answer: No, this skill only covers migration from the deprecated SDK. Use the azure-communication-callautomation-java skill for new features.
